premise: I raided my $110,000 IRA to pay off my son and daughter-in-law’s mortgage so they wouldn’t lose their home, with a binding contract to build me a basement apartment for life; they instead refinanced, pulled $200,000 in equity, and evicted me. I sued for specific performance and elder abuse, won the house free and clear, and now I live on the upper floor while my son and his wife rent the basement from me, with their eviction notice framed on the wall.
